items.front: Domestic CPDLC: when should free text be used instead of a standard message element?
items.back: Use free text only when the message content does not conform to an available standard message element. If a structured route, altitude, crossing, speed, or other element fits, use the structured element.

items.front: TBL 14-2-6: which altitude uplink family means descend to an altitude and maintain it?
items.back: UM23: descend to the specified altitude and maintain it.

items.front: TBL 14-2-8: which crossing-constraint family combines altitude and speed at the same fix?
items.back: CROSS (position) AT AND MAINTAIN (altitude) AT (speed).

items.front: Oceanic CPDLC: what is the first sorting decision for route, lateral, altitude, or speed message tables?
items.back: Message direction: controller-to-aircraft uses the uplink table; aircraft-to-controller uses the downlink table.

items.front: TBL 14-3-5: what does BACK ON ROUTE report?
items.back: The aircraft has rejoined the cleared route.

items.front: TBL 14-3-6: what does AT PILOTS DISCRETION add to an altitude assignment?
items.back: The pilot may start the climb or descent when prepared and at any rate, but once an altitude is vacated the aircraft may not return to it.

items.front: Initial departure separation: diverging courses immediately after takeoff
items.back: 1 minute until courses diverge.

items.front: Initial departure separation: diverging courses within 5 minutes after takeoff
items.back: 2 minutes until courses diverge.

items.front: Initial departure separation: diverging courses within 13 miles DME/ATD after takeoff
items.back: 3 miles until courses diverge.

items.front: Terminal nonintersecting diverging runways: 15 to 29 degrees inclusive
items.back: Simultaneous takeoffs require at least 2,000 feet between runway centerlines at and beyond the takeoff start points.

items.front: Terminal intersecting diverging runways: 15 to 29 degrees inclusive
items.back: The preceding aircraft must have passed the runway intersection and commenced a turn.

items.front: Longitudinal separation: leading aircraft at least 44 knots faster
items.back: 5 miles for DME/ATD cases, or 3 minutes between other aircraft, when one of the listed same/converging/crossing-course conditions is met.

items.front: Longitudinal separation: leading aircraft at least 22 knots faster
items.back: 10 miles for DME/ATD cases, or 5 minutes between other aircraft, when one of the listed same/converging/crossing-course conditions is met.

items.front: Longitudinal altitude-crossing minimum: required sequence geometry
items.back: The descending aircraft is leading, or the climbing aircraft is following.

items.front: Longitudinal fallback when special speed or altitude-crossing conditions are not met
items.back: 20 miles for DME/ATD cases, or 10 minutes between other aircraft.

items.front: Intersecting runway departure: preceding departure condition before takeoff roll
items.back: The preceding aircraft has passed the intersection or is turning to avert conflict.

items.front: Intersecting runway departure: preceding arrival conditions before takeoff roll
items.back: The preceding arrival is clear of the landing runway, has completed landing roll and will hold short or exit before the intersection, is observed turning before the intersection, or has passed the intersection.

items.front: Intersecting runway arrival: preceding departure condition before the arrival crosses threshold or flight path
items.back: The preceding departure has passed the intersection or flight path, or is airborne and turning to avert conflict.

items.front: LAHSO with simultaneous landings: which aircraft should be restricted if no operational benefit is lost?
items.back: Restrict the aircraft of the lesser weight category, if known.

items.front: Simultaneous helicopter or powered-lift landings/takeoffs: spacing and course conditions
items.back: At least 200 feet between landing or takeoff points, and the courses to be flown must not conflict.

items.front: Sea-lane arrival behind landed aircraft: Category II behind Category I or II
items.back: 2,500 feet from the landing threshold, between sunrise and sunset when suitable landmarks allow distance determination.

items.front: Sea-lane arrival behind airborne departure: distance when either aircraft is Category III
items.back: 6,000 feet from the landing threshold when suitable landmarks allow distance determination.
